assertions.reason: Real gh gist create/view flow in isolated temp context produced a private gist and persisted gist ID in isolated config evidence.

Backend machine-name persistence into gist payload is source-supported and partially evidenced by isolated config + gist payload, but required post-relaunch UI/screenshot verification is not fully exercisable on the tauri-rust-cli surface used here.

Source shows immediate push plus periodic timer and disable cleanup, but faithfully proving timer-driven periodic updates and stop-after-toggle requires a running Tauri app session over time; not fully user-surface testable from isolated CLI checks alone.
Toggle persistence across app restarts depends on real Tauri UI/runtime restart behavior, which was not available on this tauri-rust-cli validation surface.
Using the same private gist ID, a second isolated machine payload was merged without removing the first machine entry.
Isolated repo metadata written into the beacon payload matched the recorded branch/ahead-behind/commit metadata evidence.

Authenticated-gh error path is source-covered, but the exact missing-gh / unauthenticated user-facing toggle error could not be fully exercised through the assigned surface without launching the Tauri UI. PATH-only simulation was insufficient because cargo itself remained invocable by absolute path and test coverage does not hit the command path.
External gist deletion was confirmed via gh returning not found, but the required Tauri UI error/offer-to-recreate flow could not be observed on this CLI-only surface.
Interleaved writes preserved both machine entries in the shared gist, matching the backend read-modify-write merge behavior and automated merge test coverage.

Automated cargo coverage verifies post-rebase push failures are labeled with rebase context, and an isolated git fixture showed a successful rebase followed by a failed push while the rebased local state remained intact and retryable.

frictions.description: The assigned surface was CLI/backend-focused, so assertions requiring toggle/restart/error-toast screenshots in a live Tauri window could only be partially validated and were marked blocked instead of inferred.
gh gist CLI semantics required using actual file basenames and edit flags exactly; initial attempts with alternate argument forms failed before evidence capture succeeded.

blockers.description: No practical live Tauri desktop session was used in this flow, so restart-persistence and user-visible error/toggle assertions could not be fully exercised.
